interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, working with HR / recruiting domain
codingTopics: Data modeling and OO design: jobs, candidates, scorecards
Scheduling and intervals: interview-slot proposals across time zones
Hash maps and dedupe: candidate matching across sources
Graph and dependency: workflow stage DAGs
String and search: candidate search with filters
systemDesignTopics: Design a multi-tenant applicant tracking system with role-based permissions and customizable workflows
Design an interview scheduling engine that proposes slots across candidate and interviewer time zones
Design an integration framework connecting 200+ third-party HR tools
Design a candidate search system with complex filters over millions of records per tenant

coding: Given interviewers' calendars as busy intervals across time zones, propose the earliest common one-hour slot for a panel. Discuss interval merging.
Model a hiring workflow as a DAG of stages with entry and exit conditions. Detect cycles and compute a valid stage order via topological sort.
Deduplicate candidate records arriving from multiple sources using fuzzy matching on name and email. Discuss the clustering approach.
Implement a filter query over candidates supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you built for a domain such as HR or recruiting that you weren't personally expert in.
Describe a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant data-isolation or permissions problem.
Give an example of prioritizing customer needs when scoping an integration.
Tell me about a time you improved a slow or confusing workflow for users.
systemDesign: Design a multi-tenant ATS with role-based permissions and customizable hiring workflows.
Design a scheduling engine that proposes interview slots across candidate and interviewer time zones.
Design an integration framework that connects 200+ third-party HR and background-check tools.
values: Greenhouse champions fair, structured hiring. Describe a time you designed something to reduce bias or inconsistency.
How do you balance customizability for enterprise customers against product simplicity?
Tell me about a time you advocated for the end user, a candidate or a recruiter, in a design debate.
other: How does Greenhouse compare to Lever or Workday Recruiting?

rawSummary:
Greenhouse Interview Guide (2026): Recruiting and ATS Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Greenhouse
greenhouse.io ↗

Greenhouse is the leading enterprise ATS (Applicant Tracking System) — used by Airbnb, Stripe, Pinterest, and thousands more for hiring workflows. The interview is enterprise-SaaS-flavored with strong emphasis on multi-tenant architecture, complex data modeling (jobs, candidates, interviews, scorecards), and integrations with hundreds of third-party tools.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a multi-tenant ATS with role-based permissions and customizable workflows

- Design a scheduling engine that proposes interview slots considering candidates' time zones and interviewers' calendars

- Design integrations with 200+ third-party tools (HRIS, background checks, video conferencing)

- Coding: medium DSA, often with data-modeling flavor

- Behavioral: customer focus, working with HR / recruiting domain

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $210K–$280K

- Staff: $300K–$400K

- Principal: $420K–$550K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand SaaS multi-tenancy patterns and SOC 2 compliance basics

- Brush up on calendar / scheduling algorithms and time-zone math

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Greenhouse remote-friendly?

Hybrid in NYC (HQ), Denver, Toronto, Dublin. Many engineering roles fully remote within supported countries.

How does Greenhouse compare to Lever or Workday Recruiting?

Greenhouse is the dominant mid-market and enterprise ATS. Lever is a smaller competitor. Workday Recruiting is part of the broader Workday HRIS suite. Greenhouse pays slightly above Lever.

What is the engineering culture?

Steady, customer-focused, healthy work-life balance. Strong written communication culture. Pragmatic over flashy.
